nativeRange.habitat: Riversides, pond edges, and seasonally wet marshy areas in the southeastern United States

summary: A fine-leaved North American stem plant native to the southeastern United States, prized for needle-like foliage that flushes brilliant red to orange-red under high light, CO2, and iron-rich fertilization. Works as a colourful background or upper-midground accent in planted tanks.
careNotes: Ludwigia arcuata is a US-native stem plant (southeastern United States) in the Onagraceae family, known in the hobby for very narrow, linear, slightly arching leaves and intense red-to-orange submersed coloration. Sources broadly agree the plant is fairly forgiving of water chemistry — Flowgrow cites pH 4.5-7 and 2-15 dKH, Buce Plant cites pH 6.5-7.5; range averaged here to pH 5.5-7.5 and gH/kH 2-15. Temperature tolerance is wide (Flowgrow lists 12-28 °C with an optimum of 24-26 °C); a typical tropical-tank range of 20-28 °C is used here. Submersed height varies with light and CO2: Buce Plant cites 10-20 inches (~25-50 cm) and Flowgrow describes it as a fast grower suited to mid-background placement — adopted as 25-50 cm. The defining husbandry variable is light and iron: medium light keeps the plant alive but leaves stay green/orange, while high light combined with CO2 injection and iron-rich micronutrient dosing produces the bright red coloration the species is sold for. CO2 is technically optional (Buce Plant and Flowgrow both note the plant can grow without it) but is effectively required for deep colour and dense, compact growth — coded as 'recommended' here. Cross-source disagreement on care level: Buce Plant rates it 'easy', Flowgrow rates it 'medium', Aquarium Plants Factory implies hardy/versatile — averaged to 'intermediate' here since reliable red colour and form realistically need CO2 plus high light, even though the plant itself tolerates a wide range of conditions. The plant is fragile and sensitive to drastic parameter swings, which can trigger melt or stem rot, especially on freshly planted tissue-culture material transitioning from emersed to submersed growth. Best planted in groups of 6 or more stems to read as a bushy red accent rather than scattered single stems. Emersed growth produces rounder, greener leaves; the submersed form is the narrow-needle-leaf habit typically pictured.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings: trim healthy tops or lateral side shoots and replant directly into nutrient-rich substrate. The cut mother stem will usually branch and produce multiple new lateral shoots, allowing dense red bushes to be built up over time from a small starter group.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Used for common name (Needle Leaf Ludwigia), family (Onagraceae), origin (North America), pH (6.5-7.5), height (10-20 inches / ~25-50 cm), lighting (medium-high), CO2 (not necessary but supplementation enhances growth/colour), growth rate (moderate), placement (mid- to background), substrate (quality aquarium soil), fertilization (iron supplementation for red tones), care level (easy), propagation (stem cuttings), and notes on fragility and sensitivity to parameter swings.

additional.name: Flowgrow
additional.notes: Used for full water-parameter ranges (temperature 12-28 °C with 24-26 °C optimum, pH 4.5-7, hardness 2-15 dKH), native range (southeastern USA along rivers and pond edges), CO2 (20-40 mg/L, optional), growth rate (fast), placement (red accent, midground, nano), recommended grouping (6+ stems), propagation (lateral shoots and top cuttings), difficulty (medium), and coloration notes (reddish stem, red/golden leaves under good light and micronutrients). Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ludwigia_arcuata) returned 404 and was abandoned per fail-fast policy; Flowgrow filled the secondary slot.

additional.name: Aquarium Plants Factory
additional.notes: Cross-checked common names (Needle Leaf Ludwigia, Narrow Leaf Repens), family (Onagraceae), leaf shape (very narrow, linear, arching), submersed coloration (brilliant red to orange-red under strong lighting), placement (midground to background), lighting (medium-high), CO2 (recommended), growth rate (fast), substrate (nutrient substrate required), and propagation (cut stems and replant). Modern Aquarium (https://www.modernaquarium.com/) returned 404 for the targeted care-guide URL and was abandoned per fail-fast policy.
